extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a can only be set true by r4 (priority 50): IF (hyperkalemia AND bradycardia AND low_ejection_fraction). rec_drug_b can only be set true by r5 (priority 50): IF hyperkalemia. When hyperkalemia=true, bradycardia=true, and low_ejection_fraction=true, r4 fires setting rec_drug_a=true, and r5 also fires setting rec_drug_b=true. No competing rules exist for either output (each output has exactly one true-setting rule), so priorities and tie-breaking do not prevent both from becoming true, and the default only applies when an output is left unset. Thus rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. The remaining inputs (pregnancy, seizure_history, ckd_dialysis) only affect rec_drug_c and are set false. Note that the antecedent of r5 (hyperkalemia) is logically implied by the antecedent of r4, so any input that triggers rec_drug_a necessarily also triggers rec_drug_b — the violation is unavoidable whenever drug a is recommended.
